ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'tea beverage' class (FOODON_03301103). No specific entry exists for Teh O Poh (weak sweetened black tea, no milk). Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR covers black tea as a beverage category. Teh O Poh is a diluted, sweetened variant; closest parent applied. No exact FoodEx2 code for this Singaporean preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 16313001 refers to tea as a substance. No specific code for Teh O Poh. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard kopitiam cup of Teh O Poh is approximately 200 ml, served hot in a ceramic or glass cup.

nutrient_source.title: Teh O (Black Tea with Sugar, No Milk) — HPB Singapore Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo lists Teh O (black tea with sugar, no milk) as a standard kopitiam beverage. Teh O Poh is the weaker ('poh' = diluted) variant. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Teh O data, with sugar content proportionally reduced to reflect the diluted preparation. Energy and carbohydrate values are adjusted downward to reflect approximately 60–70% of standard Teh O sugar concentration, consistent with kopitiam practice for 'poh' orders. Protein and fat remain effectively zero as in the standard preparation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Sweetened Black Tea — analogous estimate based on sucrose-sweetened tea data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Teh O Poh in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI is estimated at 45 based on the GI of sucrose-sweetened black tea without milk, which is moderated by the tea polyphenols and the diluted sugar concentration. Sucrose has a GI of approximately 65, but when dissolved in tea at low concentration the effective GI of the beverage is lower. A GI of 45 (low category) is a defensible analogous estimate consistent with published data on lightly sweetened tea beverages. GL per serving = (3.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 200 g serving / 100) × 45 / 100 = 3.15, rounded to 3.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~4 mg per 200 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Teh O Poh is suitable for individuals with hypertension as it contains negligible sodium; however, the added sugar should be monitored if consumed multiple times daily.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar contributing approximately 7 g of simple carbohydrate per 200 ml serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request reduced or no sugar ('siu dai' or 'kosong') when ordering Teh O Poh, as the standard preparation contains added sucrose that raises postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, and presence of tea polyphenols with antioxidant properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Teh O Poh is a low-fat, cholesterol-free beverage; black tea polyphenols may confer modest cardioprotective benefits, though the added sugar should be minimised for optimal cardiovascular health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Teh O Poh (also written Tea Poh or Teh O Poh) is a traditional Singaporean kopitiam beverage consisting of weak black tea brewed with less tea leaves or more water than standard, served without milk and sweetened with a modest amount of sugar. The term 'Teh O' in Hokkien-influenced Singaporean coffeeshop dialect means black tea without milk, while 'Poh' (薄, meaning thin or diluted) indicates a weaker, more diluted brew. It is a lighter, less astringent version of the standard Teh O. The beverage is pale amber in colour, mildly sweet, and has a delicate tea flavour with low tannin intensity. It is commonly consumed at breakfast or as an afternoon drink at kopitiams (traditional coffeeshops) across Singapore and Malaysia. Nutritionally, Teh O Poh is a low-calorie beverage providing approximately 28 kcal per 200 ml serving, with negligible protein and fat. The primary macronutrient is sugar (approximately 7 g per serving) from the added sucrose. It contains trace amounts of potassium and iron from the tea infusion. Black tea polyphenols, including theaflavins and thearubigins, are present and may offer antioxidant benefits. For individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, requesting reduced sugar ('siu dai') or no sugar ('kosong') is advisable. The beverage is dairy-free, gluten-free, and suitable for vegetarian and vegan diets.

confidence_reason: HPB Singapore Foodo provides data for standard Teh O (black tea with sugar, no milk). Teh O Poh is the weaker diluted variant; nutrient values are adapted by proportional reduction of sugar content. No exact entry for Teh O Poh exists in HPB Foodo or other approved primary sources, so confidence is medium rather than high.
notes: 1. protein_g is naturally zero as black tea brewed without milk contains no measurable protein. 2. fat_g is naturally zero as this beverage contains no fat-bearing ingredients. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero for this preparation. 4. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as tea infusion contains no dietary fibre in the liquid. 5. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB source for this beverage category. 6. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB source for this beverage category. 7. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB source for this beverage category. 8. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB source for this beverage category. 9. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB source for this beverage category. 10. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB source for this beverage category. 11. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB source for this beverage category. 12. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB source for this beverage category. 13. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB source for this beverage category. 14. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB source for this beverage category. 15. glycemic_index set to 45 (analogous estimate, low category) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for sucrose-sweetened black tea without milk; no direct GI entry exists for Teh O Poh. 16. Input cuisine tag 'Italian' is incorrect for this dish; corrected to 'Singaporean' based on food identity and origin. 17. The 'poh' modifier indicates a diluted brew; sugar content is estimated at approximately 60–70% of standard Teh O to reflect this preparation.
